## [00:00 - 01:31]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about a different way to look at your Facebook ads budget. Let's go. You're looking at your Facebook ads budget all wrong. Now this is what nearly every advertiser does. We start with a low budget to test and then we get great results. So of course we then want to increase our budget to scale those results. But we know that if we do that we're taking a huge risk. You increase that budget, you're likely to restart the learning phase and you probably won't get those great results back. And no one wants that. I truly believe we need to shift how we look at the Facebook ads budget. Instead of testing low, why not test at the budget we plan to increase to if all goes well? And I understand all that resistance. But this doesn't mean we're going to waste a bunch of money. We can pull back on that budget if the results aren't what we want. And with a higher budget we also put ourselves in the best possible position to properly optimize. And then at that point we don't need to worry about increasing the budget if we're getting great results. We're already where we want to be. Too obvious? What do you think? Follow for more. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
